focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 10

Draco made tea at six in the morning because the enchanted kettle spat lukewarm water twice and because explaining that to Granger felt unreasonably close to admitting that the Manor was failing under his hands one ordinary domestic charm at a time.

He set the Muggle kettle on the hob instead. The flame answered at once. That, lately, counted as cooperation.

He didn't think about the drawing room.

He spooned tea into the pot, poured the water when it boiled, and kept his eyes on the steam.

He thought about the drawing room.

What he'd said in it. What he had not said. The way Granger's voice had gone precise enough to cut, as if precision were the only thing standing between her and something much worse. He had stood there with every possible sentence available to him and chosen a defence so thin it had shamed him while he was still speaking.

On the stairs the third tread complained, then the seventh. She came into the kitchen in a jumper that swallowed one shoulder and hair that had lost the argument with sleep.

She should not have been awake. She worked until one or two most nights and treated dawn like a private insult.

"Morning," she said.

"Morning."

Her gaze went to the kettle on the hob. Then to the dead enchanted one cooling uselessly beside it.

"Right," she said.

He reached for the teapot before she could reach for the question. "There's enough for two."

"Thanks."

She took down a mug, found the milk, and stood beside him while he poured. Not close enough to touch. Close enough that the sleeve of her jumper brushed his wrist when she reached for the sugar she wasn't going to use.

Neither of them mentioned it.

She wrapped both hands around the mug and leaned back against the counter. He took his tea to the table. After a second she cam...
open_hooks_to_carry: Hermione's guard lowering — small moment of unguarded interiority before the room appears
The drawing room: specific, sensory, the trauma preserved in merciless detail — chandelier, marble, cold
The reckoning: not clean. She tells him what she remembers. He tells her what he remembers. Neither handles it well. Unfair things said. Inadequate things said. They leave separately

payoffs_to_land.description: The room Draco has been avoiding manifests for Hermione at maximum vulnerability. The emotional geography of the Manor made concrete

prev_tail_excerpt:
.... Outside the high windows the light failed completely and the library turned into fire, shadow, and the pale geometry of shelves. The house made its small evening sounds around them. Somewhere a pipe knocked once behind the wall. Somewhere farther off a door eased shut and held.

Granger tucked one bare foot under herself in the chair, a posture he had never seen her take in the study and probably would not have seen there even on a better day. The gesture was intimate only because it was unconscious. She was too tired to maintain the full professional shape of herself.

He realized, not for the first time and not with any particular gratitude, that the afternoon had destroyed the last plausible version of the old arrangement. She could no longer touch his arm and pretend it was only diagnostic. He could no longer submit to her hands and pretend he was only being useful. Whatever happened next with the research would have to make room for the fact that she had been inside the worst moment of his life and had come back carrying it in her own nerves.

That did not make them kinder to each other by magic. It did not make the work simpler. It did not excuse a single thing that had happened in the room at sixteen or in the years after.

It did, however, make leaving harder than staying.

So he remained in the chair while the tea cooled and the fire settled lower. Hermione sat across from him with her own cup warming in both hands, her book unread, the same ugly knowledge lodged under her skin now. They did not try to return the evening to procedure. They did not promise each other anything about tomorrow. The quiet between them was no longer careful distance. It was the quiet of two people who had been inside the same pain and had not yet found a way to stand anywhere else.
state_deltas_required: The drawing room appears — the room where Hermione was tortured and Draco watched — at the moment she is closest to feeling safe in the Manor
The war's worst moment between them is on the table. The drawing room cannot be pretended away. Civil distance is no longer available
